nodes.title: Enable    Included CLIP & Included Audio VAE
CLIP Text Encode (Prompt) Negative
widgets_values: background music, noise, white noise, bleep, still image, bad quality, subtitles, text, watermark, overlay effects, pc game, yelling, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly, text, blur, logo, wordmark, static, low quality, censoring, censor, bleeping, beep, beeping, newscast, interview, podcast,  mutant, horror, slow-motion

Model Area:
 - Enable the necessary nodes depending on the checkpoint 
 type. The nodes to enable are listed for each of the three 
 model types on the left.

Distilled Lora: 
 - Please enable this option when using models that utilize 
 Distilled LoRA (such as 10Eros v1.2 or Dasiwa Ltx2.3 V4). For
 models that utilize DMD LoRA (such as 10Eros v1.4), please 
 disable Distilled LoRA and enable DMD LoRA via the Power LoRA 
 Loader in the Input area.

Sigmas & Basic Scheduler (Select only one)embedding:, 
 - If you wish to use manual sigma values ​​(for 10Eros), enable 
 "Sigmas"; if you wish to specify a custom number of steps, enable 
 "Basic Scheduler." Please enable only one of these options.

IC-Lora:
 - Please enable this if you are using the Director's "Ic-Lora" 
 feature. Be sure to disable it when performing standard 
 generation.

Sage Attention & Torch Settings:
 - Enable Sage Attention if it is installed. This may 
 improve generation speed.
 If it is not installed, disable it as an error will occur.
 Torch settings are optional.

Final Upscale:
 - Note that I have provided two types of upscaling nodes;
  if you are using an Nvidia RTX GPU, use the RTX node; 
  otherwise, replace it with a general upscaler or another
  one.

RIFE Frame Interpolation:
 - This node performs frame interpolation. Enable it if
 you wish to use it. In that case, enter the initial
 frame rate in "Base Frame Rate" on the lower side, and 
 the desired frame rate after frame interpolation in 
 "Target Frame Rate".
  If you do not wish to use it, please disable "RIFE~" and 
 enable "Don't Use RIFE".

IC Lora Strength:
 - Specify the IC LoRA weights.

Audio Volume:
 - The output volume may vary depending on the model used, so  
 you can adjust it here. A setting of +6 doubles the volume, 
 while -6 halves it.

Power LoRa Loader:
 - Ltx2.3 is very sensitive  to LoRa type and weight,so the 
 setup can be very difficult. We recommend experimenting with 
 different approaches.If you are using DMD Lora, please enable 
 it here.

Target Frame Rate:
 - For the target frame rate, if you are performing 
 frame interpolation using the RIFE Frame Interpolation node
 at the end of generation, specify the target number of 
 frames you want to output. If you are not using RIFE, no 
 setting is required.

Final Upscale Width & Height:
 - Specify the target resolution when using the final upscale.

Basic Scheduler First & Upscale Steps:
 - Specifies the step value for the Basic Scheduler. You do not 
 need to specify this if you are using a manual Sigma value.

Distilled Lora Strength First & Upscale:
 - Specify the strength of the Distilled LoRA for the First Pass
 and Upscale Pass, respectively. Note that this setting applies 
 only to video; audio settings cannot be changed here.

 (Example) 
　Dasiwa Dragonleap v4 … First 0.3～0.5  Upscale 0.3～0.5
　10Eros v1.2 … First 0.8～0.9  Upscale 0.5～0.6
  10Eros v1.4 … The use of DMD Lora is recommended. 
                No configuration is required.　　


<Director>

start_end_second & duration_seconds:
 - Specify the video duration. Typically, set `start` to "0" and 
 `end` to the desired duration in seconds; specifying 
 `duration_second` will also automatically configure the 
 corresponding settings.

frame_rate:
 - Specify the frame rate. While Ltx2.3 supports 24, 25, 48, and 
 50 fps, generating at the lower rates (24 or 25 fps) and then
 using RIFE for frame interpolation is far faster, given the 
 heavy processing load involved in generation.

custom width & height:
 - Specify the video resolution. Entering "0" applies the 
 original image's value as-is.

MAIN:
 - Upload your images using "Add Image".Select each segment and 
 enter the prompt to apply to each at the bottom.The length of 
 each segment can be freely adjusted visually.This allows you to 
 apply different prompts at specified time intervals.
 To delete a segment, click "Delete" in the bottom right.
 The length of each segment must be appropriately specified 
 according to the input prompt.
 For example,if you only specify a 2-second segment when the 
 action instructed by the prompt clearly takes more than 5 
 seconds,the output may not be as intended.
 If you want to use the same image as the previous scene and 
 only change the prompt, insert text using "Add Text".

Guide strength:
 - Set the weight for each inserted image. Specify this for 
 each segment. Generally, a weight of "0.8 to 1.0" is good for 
 the first and last images, and "0.3 to 0.5" for the 
 intermediate images, but feel free to experiment.

AUDIO & IC Lora:
 - This is used when editing videos using the custom audio 
 addition feature and the IC LoRA.

Global Prompt:
 - Enter the prompt you want to apply to the entire video.
 Specify the details for each segment.

For more details, please see here: 

   https://github.com/WhatDreamsCost/WhatDreamsCost-ComfyUI

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
